goal_99.config.title: A catchy title for your story
goal_99.config.abstract: The abstract of the chapter. In this part, please provide a brief summary of your chapter (about 50 words). Lorem ipsum odor amet, consectetuer adipiscing elit. Facilisi convallis fringilla elit dapibus mauris risus curae. Facilisis porta eros mus penatibus, tincidunt a. Cras vehicula quis viverra ridiculus convallis volutpat nam molestie ligula. Phasellus venenatis euismod in cras id porttitor; bibendum dignissim.
goal_99.config.citation: Mahler, Daniel Gerszon, and Maarten Lambrechts. 2023. “Progress, challenges in ending extreme poverty” In Atlas of Sustainable Development Goals 2023, edited by A. F. Pirlea, U. Serajuddin, A. Thudt, D. Wadhwa, and M. Welch. Washington, DC: World Bank. License: Creative Commons Attribution CC BY 3.0 IGO. https://doi.org/10.60616/f7gk-me57
goal_99.config.inputs: Andrés Castañeda, Carolina Diaz-Bonilla, Christoph Lakner, Dean Jolliffe, Gabriel Lara Ibara, Marta Schoch, Martha Viveros, Nishant Yonzan, Philip Wollburg, Samuel Kofi Tetteh Baah, Stephane Hallegatte
goal_99.content.#1.text: In the first paragraph, provide an introduction. You can add a heading like the one above from the SDGA 2025 Toolbar with Set Text elements > Subheading, or by changing the text style to Heading 1.
goal_99.content.#3.text: You can create it like the Subheading from the toolbar or from the Google Doc menu by picking Subheading 2 (toolbar) or Heading 2 (menu).
goal_99.content.#5.text: You can add footnotes[footnote:  hi footnote] from the Menu by choosing Insert > Page elements > Footnotes or by pressing Ctrl + Option + F.[footnote:  On Mac use cmd + option + F to create a footnote or use the menu. This footnote was created like that!]
goal_99.content.#6.text: For references you have to create a table at the bottom of your document of the type references (like the one at the bottom of this document. After that, you can add your references there. You can use the keys you create in the first column to create references in the text like this [reference: foster2025]. Please make sure there is no space between the last word and the [.
goal_99.content.#7.text: You can add links anywhere in the text (in references, footnotes, scene texts etc.). Simply select the text you want to turn into a link and select Insert Link from the context Menu or the top menu.
goal_99.content.#9.text: You can use Factoids to highlight important numbers in your story. Please only use them when the fact you want to highlight is a number. If you want to highlight text or a key sentence, there you can use the Emphasis from the toolbar. See explanation below.
goal_99.content.#10.text: Here is an example for a factoid:
goal_99.content.#11.title: International poverty line
goal_99.content.#11.label: per day
goal_99.content.#12.text: You can add it by choosing Insert Component > Other components > Factoid and press Insert component from the toolbar. The properties should be filled out as follows:
goal_99.content.#13.text: [emphasis: title]: the main title (usually the indicator title)
goal_99.content.#14.text: [emphasis: number]: the value you want to highlight
goal_99.content.#16.text: [emphasis: label]: additional text e.g. the year the value was measured or other secondary information.
goal_99.content.#18.text: You can highlight key takeaways of your story by creating a callout with a text emphasis. You simply have to highlight the text and choose Set Text elements > Emphasis from the toolbar. Like this:
goal_99.content.#19.text: Of all global extreme poor, [emphasis: more than 50%] live in countries where extreme poverty has increased in the past 10 years.
goal_99.content.#20.text: You can further [emphasis: emphasise words or numbers] inside and outside of Emphasis by making them bold (just choose bold from the edit menu).
goal_99.content.#21.text: If the fact you want to highlight requires a lot of text, consider using Emphasis instead of a factoid. The Factoid below show the same text as the emphasis, but is hard to grasp at a glance:
goal_99.content.#22.title: Of all global extreme poor
goal_99.content.#22.label: live in countries where extreme poverty has increased in the past 10 years.
goal_99.content.#24.text: Visualizations and scrollers can be added from the sidebar by clicking on 'Insert visualization' or 'Insert scroller+vis'. Here are some properties you can specify:
goal_99.content.#25.text: [emphasis: title]: a title that describes the main takeaway or poses a question of what to look out for in the visualization. It should tell the reader what to watch out for.
goal_99.content.#26.text: [emphasis: subtitle ](optional): Data related details and information about the data
goal_99.content.#27.text: [emphasis: textParagraph] (optional): Here you can provide some more text to describe the visualization. This is only available for visualizations outside of scrollers.
goal_99.content.#28.text: [emphasis: source]: The source of the visualization and data
goal_99.content.#29.text: [emphasis: note] (optional): Some additional notes or disclaimers.
goal_99.content.#30.text: [emphasis: visdescription]: This will not show up on the page, but will be read by screenreaders. Provide information in addition to the other text properties above, that will help people with vision impairments, who are using a screen reader. Here you can for example say what type of chart (e.g. linechart) is shown and what trends are visible. For scrollers, this should describe the content to the entire scroller, but the scene texts will also be read by screen readers so do not repeat those.
goal_99.content.#31.text: [emphasis: visSize] (optional): if you set this property to fullScreen the visualization will be wider. If you leave out this property entirely your vis will have the default size.
goal_99.content.#34.text: If you do not specify visSize and use type: vis in the vis config table, this is what your visualization looks like.
goal_99.content.#35.title: Global poverty could increase if current trends continue
goal_99.content.#35.subtitle: Past values and projections
goal_99.content.#35.textParagraph: If all countries make progress at Korea’s pace, global extreme poverty would be reduced to 1.5% by 2050. Yet copying Korea's trajectory will be difficult. If countries manage to make progress at the pace of Brazil, Tunisia, or Turkey, there will still be modest declines, while if they make progress at the speed typically observed since 1950, there will still be around 8% poverty by 2050. Unfortunately, over the past years the speed of global poverty reduction has slowed down. If countries follow their current speed of progress, global poverty will soon start to increase.[footnote: See the Explainer for how these projections are constructed.]
goal_99.content.#35.source: World Bank Poverty and Inequality Platform, World Bank Macro and Poverty Outlook October 2025, Mahler et al. (2025a), and Mahler et al. (2025b).
goal_99.content.#35.note: This is for small notes or disclaimers
goal_99.content.#37.text: This is what a fullscreen scroller looks like instead:
goal_99.content.#38.title: Global poverty projections
goal_99.content.#38.subtitle: A fullScreen Scroller
goal_99.content.#38.source: Fullscreen scroller without particles (theme: light (alternatively: dark) World Bank Poverty and Inequality Platform, World Bank Macro and Poverty Outlook October 2025, Mahler et al. (2025a), and Mahler et al. (2025b).
goal_99.content.#38.scenes.#0.text: You can use the colors from the sidebar or use the Highlight color from the Google Docs menu with the color picker to highlight parts of your text and associate it with the visualization elements. If the color is too dark. make sure to change the text color to white too.
goal_99.content.#38.scenes.#1.text: Yet copying Korea’s trajectory will be difficult. If countries manage to make progress at the typical speed observed since 1950, there will still be around 8% poverty by 2050.
goal_99.content.#38.scenes.#2.text: Unfortunately, over the past years the speed of global poverty reduction has slowed down. If countries follow their current speed of progress, global poverty will soon start to increase.
goal_99.content.#40.text: The code will automatically detect if your visualization uses particles. Here is a particle scroller visualization as an example:
goal_99.content.#41.title: Affordability of a diet depends on cost and income
goal_99.content.#41.subtitle: Cost (PPP $) for energy sufficient, nutrient-adequate and healthy diets
goal_99.content.#41.scenes.#0.text: In 1990, nearly [emphasis: 2 billion people] worldwide lived in extreme poverty – in other words, on less than $2.15 per day.
goal_99.content.#41.scenes.#2.text: Over the same period, the world’s population increased by around 2.4 billion people and the number of people with incomes above the international poverty line [emphasis: increased from 3.3 billion to 7 billion]. As a result, the share of the global population living in extreme poverty was reduced from about 38 percent to 8.5 percent.
goal_99.content.#43.text: Wrap up your story with a nice conclusion. It does not have to have this title, but it should summarize the takeaways again.
goal_99.references.foster2025: Foster, Elizabeth ⓡ Dean Jolliffe ⓡ Gabriel Lara Ibarra ⓡ Christoph Lakner ⓡ Samuel Tetteh Baah. 2025. “Global Poverty Revisted Using 2021 PPPs and New Data on Consumption.” World Bank Policy Research Working Paper no. 11137.
goal_99.references.mahler2025a: Mahler, Daniel Gerszon, Umar Serajuddin, Dviyanshi Wadhwa, Nishant Yonzan. 2025. “The World Is Developing at Its Slowest Pace in 75 Years.”
goal_99.references.mahler2025b: Mahler, Daniel Gerszon; Foster, Elizabeth; Lakner, Christoph; Prinsloo, Zander; Tchouakam Mbouendeu, Rostand; Tetteh-Baah, Samuel K.. 2025. Constructing Comparable Global Poverty Trends. Global Poverty Monitoring Technical Note; No. 45. © World Bank.

goal_99.references.berg2012: Berg, Andrew, Jonathan D. Ostry, and Jeromin Zettelmeyer. 2012. "What Makes Growth Sustained?." Journal of Development Economics 98 (2): 149-166.
goal_99.references.hausmann2005: Hausmann, Ricardo, Lant Pritchett, and Dani Rodrik. 2005. "Growth Accelerations." Journal of Economic Growth 10: 303-329.

goal_99.references.worldbank2025b: World Bank 2025. “State of Social Protection Report 2025: The 2-Billion Person Challenge.” Washington, D.C.: World Bank Group.
goal_99.references.corral2020: Corral, Paul, Alexander Irwin, Nandini Krishnan, Daniel Gerszon Mahler, and Tara Vishwanath. “Fragility and Conflict: On the Front Lines of the Fight Against Poverty.” World Bank Publications, 2020.
goal_99.references.worldbank2017: World Bank 2017. “World Development Report 2017: Governance and the Law.” Washington, D.C.: World Bank Group.
